channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Elon Musk Seemingly Admits xAI Has Used OpenAI's Models to Train Its Own
item.description: While answering questions under oath, Musk argued it’s standard practice for AI labs to use their competitors' models.

item.title: Rendering Is a Browser Decision, Not a JavaScript One
item.description: This is the fifth article in a series on how JavaScript actually runs. You can read the full series here or on my website. You change the DOM. You expect the screen to update. It doesn’t. Why? In...

item.title: Prompt Engineering Is a Real Skill, Here's the Enterprise Playbook (Not Just 'Be Specific')
item.description: System prompt architecture, few-shot design patterns, chain-of-thought for structured outputs, evaluation frameworks and version control. The stuff that actually matters in production. I want to...

item.title: Stop Building Another REST API — Make It MCP-Ready From Day One
item.description: Okay, I'll say it. Every time someone kicks off a new backend project and opens a browser tab for "REST API best practices," a small part of me quietly weeps into my coffee. Not because REST is...

item.title: Meta abandons open-source Llama for proprietary Muse Spark
item.description: Article URL: https://thenewstack.io/meta-abandons-llama-spark/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47965709 Points: 7 # Comments: 0

item.title: OpenAI Rolls Out ‘Advanced’ Security Mode for At-Risk Accounts
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out Advanced Account Security for people concerned that their ChatGPT or Codex accounts could be potential targets of phishing attacks.

item.title: Traditional Database vs Solana Accounts Comparison
item.description: Concept Traditional Database Solana Accounts Data Location Rows in tables on a centralized server Accounts on a distributed ledger across validators Schema Defined by the database (SQL DDL,...

item.title: The CORE Agentic Workflow: Task Plan Review Approve PR
item.description: The standard agentic loop — give the agent a task, get code back — has no checkpoint between your intent and the agent's execution. You find out what the agent decided to do by reading what it...

item.title: I Built a Free Quran Web App with the SERN Stack — Here's What I Learned
item.description: I Built a Free Quran Web App with the SERN Stack — Here's What I Learned A few months ago, I asked myself a simple question: Why isn't there a clean, fast, ad-free Quran web app built with modern...

item.title: Preparing RAG pipeline for production
item.description: Intro Having a working RAG that provides correct semantic answers is a great start, yet, like with every other software, the next step is to ensure the solution is safe, optimized, and keeps your...

item.title: I Taught My AI Assistant to Remember (And Saved 99% of Its Brain)
item.description: The Problem: Your AI Is a Goldfish Here's a scene that plays out in my terminal every single day: Me: "Hey AI, what's the architecture of this project?" AI: runs ls, runs find, runs grep, reads 15...

item.title: O que acontece QUANDO você clica na Internet? Desvendando a mágica!
item.description: Gurizada, outro dia me peguei pensando na quantidade de mágica que a gente consome sem nem piscar. Sabe aqueles cliques que a gente dá na internet sem pensar duas vezes? Pois é, por trás de cada...

item.title: Stripe introduces Link, a digital wallet that autonomous AI agents can use, too
item.description: Link lets users connect cards, banks, and subscriptions, then authorize AI agents to spend securely via approval flows.

item.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: Meta is running get-rich-quick ads for its AI tools
item.description: Manus, an AI company Meta acquired for $2 billion last year is running ads promising quick, easy money with AI: Find local businesses without websites or with bad websites, have AI build them one,...

item.title: Cricut’s Joy 2 makes creating stickers easier for beginners, and it’s on sale
item.description: If you’ve ever thought about making your own stickers for Etsy or just for fun but don’t know where to begin, I’d recommend taking a look at the Cricut Joy 2. I’ve been testing one for a couple of...

item.title: PyTorch Lightning Compromised in PyPI Supply Chain Attack to Steal Credentials
item.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have managed to compromise the popular Python package Lightning to push two malicious versions to conduct credential theft. According to...

item.title: Salesforce is crowdsourcing its AI roadmap — with customers
item.description: Salesforce lets its customers lead its product roadmap with the thinking that if one enterprise customer has a problem, the others likely do too.

item.title: Microsoft’s DLSS competitor is now available on the Xbox Ally X handheld
item.description: Microsoft is starting to test its Automatic Super Resolution (Auto SR) feature with the Xbox Ally X. Auto SR first debuted on some Copilot Plus PCs nearly two years ago, improving visual quality...

item.title: Gemini is rolling out to cars with Google built-in
item.description: Google is preparing to update vehicles that have Google built-in with its Gemini AI assistant. This will be an upgrade from the current Google Assistant according to Google's announcement, and...

item.title: Here’s how the new Microsoft and OpenAI deal breaks down
item.description: Microsoft's relationship with OpenAI has always been complicated, so I expected the close partnership-turned-situationship to end in tears. After all, executive disagreements, rearranged...

item.title: This startup’s new mechanistic interpretability tool lets you debug LLMs
item.description: The San Francisco–based startup Goodfire just released a new tool, called Silico, that lets researchers and engineers peer inside an AI model and adjust its parameters—the settings that determine...

item.title: Now California’s cops can give tickets to driverless cars
item.description: Autonomous vehicles roving California's roads will no longer be immune to traffic tickets starting on July 1st. New regulations announced by the California DMV this week allow law enforcement to...

item.title: After working on the Vision Pro, this AR veteran is going back to phones
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. When Mark Drummond was working...

item.title: $100 Nintendo gift cards are $20 off at Newegg today
item.description: Nintendo eShop gift cards can be used to buy games, apparel, and even hardware or accessories from its online storefront. No matter what you want to get, you can save a fairly substantial amount...

item.title: Subnautica 2 launches soon after lengthy legal dispute
item.description: After some protracted legal drama, Subnautica 2 is finally about to launch. The game, which is currently Steam's most-wishlisted title and was caught up in a dispute between top executives at...

item.title: X announces a rebuilt ad platform powered by AI
item.description: X is rolling out a rebuilt ads platform powered by AI as it works to grow revenue again.

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: SMS Blaster Busts, OpenEMR Flaws, 600K Roblox Hacks and 25 More Stories
item.description: The internet is noisy this week. We are seeing some wild new tactics, like people using fake cell towers to send scam texts, while some developers are accidentally downloading tools that peek into...

item.title: New Python Backdoor Uses Tunneling Service to Steal Browser and Cloud Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a stealthy Python-based backdoor framework called DEEP#DOOR that comes with capabilities to establish persistent access and harvest a wide range...
